Based on the provided evidence, there seems to be variability in the exact durations mentioned for local anesthetics, particularly those falling closer to the 5-minute mark versus those extending towards nearly 20 minutes. Tetracaine's documented usage suggests it could last anywhere upwards of two to three minutes initially, aligning closely with proparacaine since both belong to the family of amino esters. Given the nature of short-duration topical anesthetics intended primarily for immediate need rather than extended periods, options such as "5 min" seem plausible based on historical application patterns described even though absolute specifics aren’t definitively stated here.
